lede: A look at April 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Maria Sharapova and Sarah Bagley, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2005 conclave
events.desc: Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger is elected to the papacy and becomes Pope Benedict XVI.

events.title: Oklahoma City bombing
events.desc: Oklahoma City bombing: The Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building in Oklahoma City, US, is bombed, killing 168 people including 19 children under the age of six.

events.title: Gun turret
events.desc: A gun turret explodes on the USS Iowa, killing 47 sailors.

events.title: Salyut 1
events.desc: Launch of Salyut 1, the first space station.

events.title: Grace Kelly
events.desc: Actress Grace Kelly marries Prince Rainier of Monaco.

events.title: Mae West
events.desc: Mae West is sentenced to ten days in jail for obscenity for her play Sex.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Baltimore riot of 1861: A pro-Secession mob in Baltimore attacks United States Army troops marching through the city.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The war begins during the Battles of Lexington and Concord with a victory of American minutemen and other militia over British forces, later referred to as the "shot heard round the world".

events.title: Charles VI, Holy Roman Emperor
events.desc: With no living male heirs, Charles VI, Holy Roman Emperor, issues the Pragmatic Sanction of 1713 to ensure that Habsburg lands and the Austrian throne would be inheritable by a female; his daughter and successor, Maria Theresa, was not born until 1717.

events.title: Lisbon massacre
events.desc: The Lisbon Massacre begins, in which about two thousand Jews who had been forcibly converted to Christianity are slaughtered by Portuguese Catholics.

births.name: Glenn T. Seaborg
births.line: American chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Konrad Adenauer, German politician, 1st Chancellor of Germany
Pierre Curie, French physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ælfheah of Canterbury (Anglican, Catholic, Orthodox)
Christian feast day: Olaus and Laurentius Petri (Lutheran)
